canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, generalization is consistently defined as a model's ability to maintain strong performance on new, unseen data, tasks, or conditions that differ from its training distribution. While this core definition is widely shared, some papers adopt more specific framings such as cross-lingual, temporal, or task-agnostic generalization to denote performance across linguistic, time-based, or task-type boundaries. Researchers operationalize and measure this construct by evaluating model performance on a wide array of out-of-distribution (OOD) benchmarks, with MMLU, GSM8k, HellaSwag, and ARC-Challenge being among the most frequently used instruments. A recurring theme is the explicit contrast between generalization and memorization, with one study noting that "Disentangling the effects of generalization and test set memorization is critical" ("Proving Test Set Contamination in Black-Box Language Models"). This is often studied alongside overfitting, where methods are assessed on their ability to improve generalization without simply memorizing training data. The construct is a common target for improvement in various contexts, including instruction tuning, which one paper describes as having the "primary purpose" of expanding generalization abilities ("OctoPack: Instruction Tuning Code Large Language Models"), as well as in research on alignment, knowledge editing, and embodied agents.

definitions.text: The ability of a model or fusion method to maintain strong performance across different conditions, datasets, or tasks, such as clean vs. noisy speech or audio-visual settings.
definitions.paper: $\mathcal{B}$-Coder: Value-Based Deep Reinforcement Learning for Program Synthesis

definitions.text: The extent to which a model maintains useful performance across diverse domains, entity types, and evaluation settings beyond the specific data it was trained on.
definitions.paper: An interpretable error correction method for enhancing code-to-code translation

definitions.text: The model's capacity to perform effectively on new, unseen data and tasks that differ from its training distribution.
definitions.paper: KoLA: Carefully Benchmarking World Knowledge of Large Language Models

definitions.text: The ability of the quantized model to preserve performance across different datasets, model sizes, and evaluation settings.
definitions.paper: AffineQuant: Affine Transformation Quantization for Large Language Models

definitions.text: The latent tendency of a trained reflector or system to transfer effectively across different actor models or settings.
definitions.paper: Reflective Multi-Agent Collaboration based on Large Language Models

definitions.text: The ability of a merged model to preserve the union of all skills from its base models and correctly classify inputs from any of the source tasks into a unified label space.
definitions.paper: Model merging with SVD to tie the Knots

definitions.text: The model's underlying strategy for applying knowledge learned from pretraining data to new, unseen problems.
definitions.paper: Procedural Knowledge in Pretraining Drives Reasoning in Large Language Models

definitions.text: The degree to which a model's capability learned in one language can be applied to another language without additional training, reflecting the generalization of skills across linguistic boundaries.
The ability to apply knowledge learned from data in one language (typically high-resource) to perform tasks in another language (typically low-resource).
The ability of a model trained or tuned in one language to effectively apply reasoning skills to other languages, especially low-resource ones, without performance degradation.
definitions.paper: Learning to Substitute Words with Model-based Score Ranking

definitions.text: The ability of a reasoning method to transfer effectively across different tasks, models, and problem domains without significant reconfiguration.
definitions.paper: ImpliHateVid: A Benchmark Dataset and Two-stage Contrastive Learning Framework for Implicit Hate Speech Detection in Videos

definitions.text: The ability to accurately verify claims that occur after the model's knowledge cutoff by relying on external evidence rather than parametric memory.
definitions.paper: DEFAME: Dynamic Evidence-based FAct-checking with Multimodal Experts

definitions.text: The capacity of a model or method to maintain performance across diverse tasks without requiring task-specific fine-tuning or retraining.
definitions.paper: Retraining-free Merging of Sparse MoE via Hierarchical Clustering

definitions.text: The ability of a model to maintain performance on tasks when the number of input items exceeds the maximum seen during training, particularly relevant for reasoning tasks over variable-length sequences.
definitions.paper: Softmax is not Enough (for Sharp Size Generalisation)

definitions.text: The capacity to apply a cognitive skill across diverse contexts and stimulus types, rather than relying on narrow, task-specific patterns or priors.
definitions.paper: Position: Principles of Animal Cognition to Improve LLM Evaluations

definitions.text: The extent to which statistical patterns in response length, such as GEV distribution fit, hold across different LLM architectures and scales, indicating a fundamental property of transformer-based generation.

characterization: Across the surveyed literature, generalization is consistently defined as a model's ability to maintain strong performance on new, unseen data, tasks, or conditions that differ from its training distribution. While this core definition is widely shared, some papers adopt more specific framings such as cross-lingual, temporal, or task-agnostic generalization to denote performance across linguistic, time-based, or task-type boundaries. Researchers operationalize and measure this construct by evaluating model performance on a wide array of out-of-distribution (OOD) benchmarks, with MMLU, GSM8k, HellaSwag, and ARC-Challenge being among the most frequently used instruments. A recurring theme is the explicit contrast between generalization and memorization, with one study noting that "Disentangling the effects of generalization and test set memorization is critical" ("Proving Test Set Contamination in Black-Box Language Models"). This is often studied alongside overfitting, where methods are assessed on their ability to improve generalization without simply memorizing training data. The construct is a common target for improvement in various contexts, including instruction tuning, which one paper describes as having the "primary purpose" of expanding generalization abilities ("OctoPack: Instruction Tuning Code Large Language Models"), as well as in research on alignment, knowledge editing, and embodied agents.
